title: GAO-09-149 (Full Report): Social Security Disability — Collection of Medical Evidence (2008)
description: Full text of GAO report GAO-09-149 (64-page PDF, December 2008), 'Social Security Disability: Collection of Medical Evidence Could Be Improved with Evaluations to Identify Promising Collection Practices', a report to the Subcommittee on Social Security, House Committee on Ways and Means. Examines challenges DDSs face collecting medical records from claimants' providers, obtaining high-quality consultative exams, and SSA's transition to electronic evidence collection, with detailed findings, scope/methodology, and SSA agency comments. Companion full-text representation of the gao-09-149 base record.

source.title: Social Security Disability: Collection of Medical Evidence Could Be Improved with Evaluations to Identify Promising Collection Practices (GAO-09-149, Full Report)

notes: Authoritative: extracted full text of the published 64-page GAO-09-149 PDF. This is the full-report representation in the gao-09-149 family (base = oversight/gao/gao-09-149, summary = oversight/gao/gao-09-149-summary). url points to GAO's full-report PDF asset. PDF-to-text extraction may contain layout artifacts (split headings, hyphenation). Single publication year so temporal_coverage start==end (2008). Narrative document, no declared column schema.
